Student Name: James Thompson
Period: 2
Date: 2/18/2025
Assignment: To Kill a Mockingbird Theme Essay

Questions

[GRAIDER:QUESTION:1]
1) State your thesis: What is a major theme in To Kill a Mockingbird and how does Harper Lee develop it? (20 pts)
In Harper Lee's novel To Kill a Mockingbird, the theme of moral courage is shown through the character of Atticus Finch who stands up for what is right even when the whole town is against him. The novel teaches readers that doing the right thing is not always easy but it is always important. This essay will explore how Atticus demonstrates courage through his defense of Tom Robinson and his teachings to his children.

[GRAIDER:QUESTION:2]
2) Provide evidence from the text that supports your thesis. Include at least 3 specific examples with quotes or detailed references. (45 pts)
Atticus Finch shows moral courage most clearly when he agrees to defend Tom Robinson, a black man accused of a crime he didn't commit. In the 1930s South, defending a black man was extremely unpopular and dangerous. Atticus tells Scout that "the one thing that doesn't abide by majority rule is a person's conscience." This quote shows that Atticus believes in doing what is right regardless of what others think.

Another example of courage in the novel is when Atticus faces the mob at the jail. He sits outside Tom Robinson's cell knowing that angry men might hurt him. He doesn't bring a weapon or call for help. Instead he uses calm words and his moral authority. When Scout arrives and talks to Mr. Cunningham, it reminds the mob that they are individuals not just an angry group.

Atticus also teaches his children about courage through the example of Mrs. Dubose. He makes Jem read to her even though she says terrible things about Atticus. After she dies Atticus explains that she was fighting a morphine addiction and that "real courage is when you know you're licked before you begin but you begin anyway." This lesson helps Jem understand that courage isn't about physical strength.

[GRAIDER:QUESTION:3]
3) Write a conclusion that connects the theme to modern relevance. (20 pts)
In conclusion, To Kill a Mockingbird shows that moral courage means standing up for your beliefs even when it is difficult and unpopular. Atticus Finch is the perfect example of this because he defends Tom Robinson, faces the mob, and teaches his children the true meaning of bravery. Harper Lee's message is still relevant today because we still need people who are willing to do the right thing even when it's hard. The novel reminds us that courage is about conscience not convenience.

GRAIDER_TABLE_V1
